name: what-the
tagline: One-line prompt skill: reframes a request as a plain-English who/what/where/why/when breakdown of something technical.
verdict: Safe to install: no tools, network, file, or shell access, nothing hidden — the whole skill is a single canned prompt line. It is extremely thin (7 lines total): no output format, no negative triggers, no example, and no failure-path guidance, so results depend entirely on the model's own judgment rather than the skill's structure.

highlights.summary: No negative-trigger guidance is given (when NOT to use it, e.g. topics needing deep technical precision instead of a plain-English pass).
Body (line 6) is one instructive sentence, not a sequence of steps — it restates the ask with no method for gathering, verifying, or organizing the explanation.
No defined output contract; the five categories (who/what/where/why/when) are named but no structure, headings, or length are specified for the answer.
